programme.title: The Devil at 4 O'Clock
programme.desc: Drama. Spencer Tracy stars as an unfairly unpopular priest living on a volcanic South Sea island which is about to erupt. With Kerwin Matthews, Frank Sinatra and Barbara Luna.

programme.title: Men in Black: International Interview Special
programme.desc: Stars Chris Hemsworth and Tessa Thompson, director F Gary Gray and producers Walter Parkes and Laurie Macdonald talk about bringing the series back for a fourth part.

programme.title: A Knight's Tale
programme.desc: Modern swashbuckling medieval action adventure starring Heath Ledger, Shannyn Sossamon and Paul Bettany. A lowly squire jousts his way to the top, with help from Geoffrey Chaucer.

programme.title: Boston Strangler
programme.desc: Keira Knightley stars as Loretta McLaughlin, the reporter who fought 60s chauvinism to break the story of the notorious titular serial killer, in Matt Ruskin's true crime procedural.

programme.title: My Friend Dahmer
programme.desc: Ross Lynch portrays serial killer Jeffrey Dahmer at high school in this biographical drama based on John 'Derf' Backderf's graphic novel.

programme.title: The Night House
programme.desc: Supernatural horror with Rebecca Hall. A woman starts to think her dead husband is haunting her as she wrestles with her grief in the isolated lakeside house he built for them.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Buchanan Rides Alone
programme.desc: Western. Randolph Scott stars as a drifter who arrives in a small border town and tangles with a group of villains after helping a wealthy young Mexican, played by Manuel Rojas.

programme.title: The Violent Men
programme.desc: Cold War-influenced western starring Glenn Ford and Edward G Robinson. A smallholder stands alone against the expansionist plans and brutal tactics of a tyrannical rancher.

programme.title: Sea Wife
programme.desc: Offbeat melodrama starring Richard Burton (as an RAF officer), Joan Collins (as a nun), Basil Sydney and Cy Grant, who end up on a life raft together after their ship is torpedoed.

programme.title: Hell and High Water
programme.desc: Cold War thriller. Richard Widmark stars as the leader of a submarine expedition, sent to the frozen depths of Alaska to thwart the Chinese communists' plans to start WWIII.

programme.title: Midway
programme.desc: Roland Emmerich's sprawling, action-packed war drama about the Battle of Midway, a major turning point in World War II. Starring Ed Skrein, Luke Evans and Woody Harrelson.

programme.title: Quadrophenia
programme.desc: Landmark cultural-snapshot drama starring Phil Daniels, Leslie Ash, Sting and Ray Winstone. Jimmy finds identity and community with the mods. But the scene has a dark side.

programme.title: This Is England
programme.desc: Shane Meadows' award-winning drama starring Thomas Turgoose. In the 1980s, a bullied 13-year-old finds friendship with a group of skinheads. But one of them is also a violent racist.

programme.title: Alita: Battle Angel
programme.desc: Partially animated action film based on a popular manga comic. Alita, a deadly cyborg brought back to life by a mourning scientist, sets out to discover her true identity.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping.

programme.title: Siege of the Saxons
programme.desc: Dark ages adventure. King Arthur is murdered and usurped by his champion knight (Ronald Howard), so an outlaw (Ronald Lewis) seeks the mystic Merlin (John Laurie) to right the wrong.

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: Terror by Night
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes murder-mystery starring Basil Rathbone and Nigel Bruce. Holmes and Dr Watson are employed to protect a priceless diamond. But it's snatched from under their noses.

programme.title: Arabesque
programme.desc: Gregory Peck stars in Stanley Donen's spy thiller. An Oxford professor is unwittingly drawn into a deadly web of political intrigue. With Sophia Loren and Alan Badel.

programme.title: Fantastic Voyage
programme.desc: Classic sci-fi adventure. Raquel Welch and Donald Pleasence are among the crew of a miniaturised submarine that's injected into an injured diplomat's body in a bid to save his life.

programme.title: Spartacus
programme.desc: 'I'm Spartacus!' Stanley Kubrick's Oscar-winning historical epic starring Kirk Douglas as the storied slave-cum-gladiator who takes on the might of imperial Rome. With Tony Curtis.
